channel.title: Bridge Events on Pittsburgh Bridge
channel.description: Recent content in Bridge Events on Pittsburgh Bridge

item.title: Munches
item.description:
Munches are social gatherings, which are generally held at restaurants. These are weeknight events which center around food, drink, and discussion with fellow kinksters. They offer a relaxed atmosphere and are an excellent way to meet people and begin to explore kink.

Pittsburgh Bridge Munch: Roland’s

7 p.m. on the first Tuesday of the month at Roland’s Seafood Grill in the Strip District.

Roland’s is one of our favorite venues. It offers a large, private area upstairs with a deck, pool tables, a huge bar, a lounge, and all the space we need to stretch out. Downstairs, where This Generation Pittsburgh is held, is a smaller, comfortable space with a cozy fireplace. Summer or winter, Roland’s is a fantastic place to enjoy an evening with friends.

item.title: Happy Hour
item.description:
If you’re not quite ready for a play event, but looking for something a little wilder than a munch, Happy Hours are the events for you. They’re held at a selection of bars across the city and generally include drinks, dancing, and a party atmosphere. Pittsburgh Bridge Happy Hour is held at 7 p.m. on the second or third Friday of the month at various bars around the city.

Pittsburgh Bridge is currently reevaluating our Happy Hour locations as we recover from the pandemic.

item.title: Education
item.description:
Bridge Academy

Generally on a Saturday from 2-4, some topics run longer. These classes are in depth looks on a variety of different topics. Classes can be structured as demonstrations, lectures, discussions, or hands-on workshops. They cover content on kink dynamics and technical skills of play.

More information on our events coming soon as we get in full swing from the pandemic.
